revision.text:
[[ja:御霊神社 (福知山市)]] {{Infobox religious building | name = Goryō Shrine | image = File:Goryo shrine frontview.jpg | location = 238 Nishinaka-no-chō, {{ill|Fukuchiyama, Kyoto|en|Fukuchiyama, Kyoto|qid=Q3355306|lt=Fukuchiyama|1=Fukuchiyama, Kyoto}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|qid=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|35.3013444|N|135.1216444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deities = [[Ukanomitama]]
{{ill|Akechi Mitsuhide|en|Akechi Mitsuhide|qid=Q313320|lt=Akechi Mitsuhide|1=Akechi Mitsuhide}} | founded = [[1705]] (Hōei 2) | annual_festival = First Sunday of October | prefecture= {{ill|Fukuchiyama|qid=Q3355306|1=Fukuchiyama, Kyoto}} | deity= {{ill|Akechi Mitsuhide|qid=Q313320|1=Akechi Mitsuhide}}; {{ill|Ukanomitama|qid=Q3080728|1=Ukanomitama}} }} '''Goryō Shrine''' (御霊神社, ''Goryō-jinja'') is a '''{{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}}''' located in '''{{ill|Fukuchiyama, Kyoto|en|Fukuchiyama, Kyoto|qid=Q3355306|lt=Fukuchiyama|1=Fukuchiyama, Kyoto}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|qid=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}''', Japan. == '''Enshrined Deities''' == The shrine enshrines: * '''[[Ukanomitama]] (宇賀御霊大神)''' – A deity of prosperity and agriculture. * '''{{ill|Akechi Mitsuhide|en|Akechi Mitsuhide|qid=Q313320|lt=Akechi Mitsuhide|1=Akechi Mitsuhide}} (明智光秀)''' – The famous {{ill|Samurai|en|Samurai|qid=Q38142|lt=Samurai|1=Samurai}} and lord of {{ill|Fukuchiyama Castle|en|Fukuchiyama Castle|qid=Q2969288|lt=Fukuchiyama Castle|1=Fukuchiyama Castle}}. == '''History''' == Goryō Shrine was founded in '''1705 (Hōei 2)'''.{{ill|Tetsuo Owada|en|Tetsuo Owada|qid=Q11459346|lt=Tetsuo Owada|1=Tetsuo Owada}} (ed.), *Akechi Mitsuhide: Hidemitsu*, Minerva Shobō, 2019, p. 240. Originally, the shrine consisted of '''two separate entities'''—one dedicated to '''Ukanomitama''', worshiped as an '''Inari shrine''', and the other, '''Jōshō-ji Temple (常照寺)''' in '''Hishiyachō''', which honored '''Akechi Mitsuhide'''.[http://jinja.kojiyama.net/?p=16442 Goryō Shrine (Fukuchiyama) - Kojiyama Shrine Records] Retrieved February 18, 2018. It is believed that '''the ruling lord of Fukuchiyama Castle, the Kutsuki clan''', permitted the merging of these two sites to honor '''Akechi Mitsuhide''', who had previously '''named the region "Fukuchiyama" and significantly modernized Fukuchiyama Castle'''. The shrine's name, '''Goryō''', reflects this historical connection. As a result, the shrine possesses '''numerous historical artifacts related to Akechi Mitsuhide'''. == '''Shrine Grounds''' == [[File:Tidemark of 1953 Fukuchiyama flood.jpg|thumb|right|240px|Flood level marker from the 1953 Fukuchiyama flood]] In '''1953 (Shōwa 28),''' '''Typhoon No. 13''' caused the '''Yura River (由良川)''' to flood, submerging much of '''Fukuchiyama'''. To commemorate this disaster, a '''water level marker''' was placed in '''Goryō Park''', adjacent to the shrine, indicating the '''flood height that once covered the city'''. == '''Festivals and Events''' == === '''Annual Festival (October)''' === The '''shrine’s annual festival''' is held on the '''first Sunday of October'''. This coincides with the '''"Tanba Mitsuhide Kikyo Festival"''' (丹波光秀ききょうまつり), one of Fukuchiyama's major autumn events. The festival takes place in '''Goryō Park and Hirokōji Street''', featuring '''performances, street vendors, and cultural displays'''. == '''Cultural Properties''' == === '''Important Cultural Properties (Fukuchiyama City Designation)''' === * '''Goryō Shrine Spears (御霊神社槍, three spears)''' ** It is said that a '''local blacksmith named Sukebe Dōmyō (雀部道明)''', who originally made farming tools, was tasked by '''Akechi Mitsuhide''' with crafting spears and arrowheads for his army. Though the craftsmanship is rough, these weapons were '''renowned for their cutting ability'''. The spears at Goryō Shrine are thought to be made by later generations of this '''local blacksmith lineage'''.[http://www.city.fukuchiyama.kyoto.jp/fukuchiyamaisanWEB/siteibunkazai/kougeihin/goryoujinjayari.htm Goryō Shrine Spears - Fukuchiyama City Cultural Heritage] Retrieved February 18, 2018. === '''Tangible Cultural Properties (Fukuchiyama City Designation)''' === * '''Goryō Shrine Akechi Mitsuhide Documents (御霊神社紙本墨書明智光秀関係文書)''' ** This collection consists of '''two letters by Akechi Mitsuhide''' and '''one volume of Akechi Mitsuhide's military laws'''. The documents date from '''Tenshō 6–9 (1578–1581)'''.[http://www.city.fukuchiyama.kyoto.jp/fukuchiyamaisanWEB/siteibunkazai/komonjyo/goryoujinjya%20mituhidekankei.html Akechi Mitsuhide Documents - Fukuchiyama City Cultural Heritage] Retrieved February 18, 2018. == '''location and Access''' == * '''Address''': 238 Nishinaka-no-chō, '''Fukuchiyama''', Kyoto Prefecture, Japan. * '''Nearest Station''': ** '''10-minute walk''' north (approx. '''800m''') from '''Fukuchiyama Station (JR West, Fukuchiyama Line)'''. === '''Surroundings''' === Goryō Shrine is located in '''Fukuchiyama’s city center''', northwest of the '''former castle town'''. The shrine and '''Goryō Park''' occupy a section of '''Hirokōji Street''', forcing the road to take a '''detour around the shrine'''. This '''historical shopping street''' features '''various traditional merchants''', and additional shopping districts such as '''Shinmachi Shopping Street''' are nearby. == '''See Also''' == * [[Goryo Shrine|Goryō Shrine]] (disambiguation) * {{ill|Fukuchiyama Castle|en|Fukuchiyama Castle|qid=Q2969288|lt=Fukuchiyama Castle|1=Fukuchiyama Castle}} == '''References''' == {{Reflist}} == '''External Links''' == * [http://www.kyoto-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/19/057/index.html Goryō Shrine - Kyoto Shrine Association] (in Japanese) {{Infobox_footer}} {{pref-stub-京都府}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Buddhism-stub}} }} {{translated page|ja|御霊神社 (福知山市)|version=95743696|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[ja:御霊神社 (福知山市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11489361}} [[Category:Akechi Mitsuhide]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Fukuchiyama, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Fukuchiyama, Kyoto]] [[Category:Inari faith]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Tanba Province]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with 50+ untranslated japanese characters]]
